title: Duplicate docker-python testing in CI
body:
/kind feature

**Description**

Recently [it was discovered](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13241#issuecomment-1042971793) that two Cirrus-CI testing tasks are executing basically the same tests.  This is likely coming from duplication across the `Makefile` targets `localapiv2` and `run-docker-py-tests` both targeting `./test/python/docker`.

*note:* I'm labeling this a feature, since nothing is actually broken, it's simply inefficient. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run CI testing on a PR

**Describe the results you received:**

Both the `APIv2 test on fedora-35` and `Docker-py Compat` CI tasks run python tests under `./test/python/docker`.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Only one of the tasks should run tests under `./test/python/docker`.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

N/A

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

N/A

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

N/A

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: @jwhonce are you aware / do you know any history or reason behind this?  Would you be okay if I dropped the one under the `localapiv2` target?
